Social Media Posts — 02 / 04

Stripped
Bare

A mock public health campaign exploring the hidden risks of commercial shampoos — created for a class project, researched and designed independently.

Mock CampaignPublic Health Gen ZCanvaAcademic
TypeMock Campaign
Handle@strippedbare
TargetGen Z + Young Millennials
ToolCanva + Pinterest
FocusIngredient Awareness

Campaign Overview

"This series of social media graphics is part of a mock public health campaign I created for a class project centered on the hidden risks of commercial shampoos. Driven by a personal interest in holistic health and ingredient awareness, I turned curiosity into a well-researched, visually compelling campaign — creating all content and designs myself. Every claim and concept was backed by credible sources included in the final written submission."

Campaign Graphics

Campaign Strategy

The Audience

Gen Z and younger Millennials — demographics highly active online, more likely to question mainstream beauty standards, explore natural alternatives, and shift consumer habits for health and sustainability. Content was designed to provoke thought and educate, not alarm.

The Approach

Multiple themed carousel series covering endocrine disruptors, alternative haircare methods, and debunking "natural" label claims. Bold typography and editorial imagery created a visual identity that felt urgent and credible rather than preachy.
